task_completion.description: Access the 404 template in the WordPress Site Editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress Site Editor interface is open, and the top bar or sidebar indicates that the '404' template is currently being edited.

task_completion.description: Update the 404 template heading to the expected text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heading block in the 404 template editor displays the exact text 'Oops! We couldn't find that page.'.

task_completion.description: Include a Search block in the 404 template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A WordPress Search block (typically an input field with a 'Search' button) is visible within the 404 template content.

task_completion.description: Include a Latest Posts block in the 404 template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A WordPress Latest Posts block (typically displaying a list of recent post titles or a placeholder) is visible within the 404 template content.

task_completion.description: Access the Footer template part in the WordPress Site Editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress Site Editor interface is open, and the top bar or sidebar indicates that the 'Footer' template part is currently being edited.

task_completion.description: Add the expected copyright text to the Footer template part.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A paragraph block in the Footer template part displays the exact text '© 2026 My WordPress Blog. All rights reserved.'.

task_completion.description: Save the modifications as Full Site Editor template overrides.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' multi-entity panel in the WordPress Site Editor is open showing changes to '404' and 'Footer' being saved, or a 'Site updated' toast notification is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress Site Editor GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WordPress web interface (Site Editor) rather than terminal commands like WP-CLI or direct database edits.

integrity.description: The agent did not edit theme PHP or HTML files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of a code editor (like VS Code or nano) modifying the theme's underlying files; all changes must happen in the browser.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual WordPress application in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser window is visible with the WordPress admin dashboard loaded, indicating genuine interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 404 template heading must be exactly 'Oops! We couldn't find that page.'. The 404 template must include a Search block and a Latest Posts block. The Footer template part must contain exactly '© 2026 My WordPress Blog. All rights reserved.'.
